Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the methyltransferase TylM1/DesVI family. {ECO:0000305}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=dTDP-3-amino-3,4,6-trideoxy-alpha-D-glucose + 2 S-adenosyl-L-methionine = dTDP-alpha-D-desosamine + 2 S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + 2 H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:31759, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:63260, ChEBI:CHEBI:63262; EC=2.1.1.234; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:12119032}; PhysiologicalDirection=left-to-right; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:31760; Evidence={ECO:0000305|PubMed:12119032};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: S-adenosyl-L-methionine-dependent methyltransferase involved in the biosynthesis of desosamine, found in certain macrolide antibiotics such as erthyromycin, azithromycin, clarithromycin, and methymycin. Catalyzes the last step in the biosynthesis of dTDP-desosamine, i.e. the N,N-dimethylation of the 3-amino group of dTDP-3-amino-3,4,6-trideoxy-alpha-D-glucose. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:12119032}.
